---
title: "Lecture 05 Notes: Robot Modeling II - Forward Kinematics"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 05 slides](../slides/05-robot-modeling-1-kinematics.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Forward kinematics maps joint configuration \(q\) to end-effector pose \(x\).
- Kinematic chains are modeled by multiplying link-by-link transforms.
- Workspace limits emerge from joint range and link geometry.

## Core relation

\[
x = f(q), \quad T_{0e}(q) = T_{01}(q_1) T_{12}(q_2) \cdots T_{(n-1)n}(q_n)
\]

## Review questions

1. What information is needed to compute forward kinematics?
2. How does joint offset calibration affect \(f(q)\)?
3. Why can different robots have similar reachable workspaces?
